claim: HS-JEPA is a general architecture for human-understanding prediction: predict hidden human-state, listener responsibility, action-health, and invariant-preserving action representations before making bounded outputs.
core_equation: partial_human_context -> hidden_human_state -> listener_responsibility -> action_health -> invariant_preserving_decoder -> anti_shortcut_validation

modules.name: Human-State Context Encoder
modules.purpose: Encode partial person, cohort, time, routine, social, and sensor context into a hidden human-state field.
modules.failure_if_removed: The model collapses into direct label prior fitting or local feature heuristics.

modules.name: Masked State Predictor
modules.purpose: Predict unobserved state/listener representations from visible context without reconstructing raw inputs.
modules.failure_if_removed: The representation no longer proves that it understands hidden human structure.

modules.name: Listener Responsibility
modules.purpose: Treat labels, sensors, surveys, or outcomes as listeners that react differently to the same human state.
modules.failure_if_removed: The architecture becomes a flat multi-label classifier with no explanation of which outcome should react.

modules.name: Action-Health Decoder
modules.purpose: Decide whether a latent signal is healthy enough to translate into an output action.
modules.failure_if_removed: Good-looking latent signals become unsafe output moves and overfit shortcut regions.

modules.name: Invariant Energy
modules.purpose: Score whether an action preserves the behavioral, physiological, temporal, or semantic manifold of the domain.
modules.failure_if_removed: The model can improve a local listener while breaking the global human-state manifold.

modules.name: Anti-Shortcut Validation
modules.purpose: Stress-test the representation and action field against nulls, cohort shifts, time shifts, and shortcut sensors.
modules.failure_if_removed: A lucky split or public-only shortcut can be mistaken for human-state understanding.

portability_gates.evidence: Core contracts use generic listeners/outcomes instead of dataset-specific target names.
Core contracts do not require leaderboard observations or submission files.
Dataset-specific invariants and output schema must be supplied by an adapter.
Each core module has a removal test and expected failure mode.
The core predicts hidden state/listener/action representations before final labels.
